assets.name: Motab Shaikh
assets.note: ADR/WB Election Watch: ₹11.55 Cr (2021) → ₹17.02 Cr (2026); +47.4% growth. Re-contested 2026; INC won only Sujapur and Hansan — constituency and outcome not confirmed in this tracker.

spotlights.name: Partha Chatterjee
spotlights.role: TMC · Education Minister 2011–2022 · Behala Paschim MLA
spotlights.desc: Between his 2016 and 2021 affidavits, Partha Chatterjee's declared assets grew from ₹22 lakh to ₹97 lakh on a minister's salary — itself suspicious. When the ED arrested him in July 2022, ₹49.8 crore in cash and 5 kg of gold were seized from the home of his close associate Arpita Mukherjee. The gap between ₹97 lakh declared and ₹49.8 crore seized is the starkest single illustration of affidavit underreporting in Bengal's political history.
spotlights.source: ED seizure records, July 2022. ADR India affidavit analysis 2016–2021.

spotlights.name: Anisur Rahaman Bidesh
spotlights.role: TMC · Deganga MLA · 4 declared criminal cases
spotlights.desc: Declared assets grew from ₹85 lakh to ₹3.40 crore — a ₹2.55 crore increase — while serving as MLA on a salary that could account for at most ₹40 lakh. Bidesh also declared four criminal cases in his 2026 affidavit, including a charge under IPC Section 307 (attempt to murder). No business income of matching scale is declared in the affidavit. This is the largest flagged growth among MLAs in this tracker's current dataset.
spotlights.source: ECI affidavit data 2021 & 2026; ADR India MyNeta.

spotlights.name: Sital Kapat
spotlights.desc: A first-time MLA in 2021 with no prior public role, Kapat declared ₹75 lakh in 2021. By 2026, this had grown to ₹1.60 crore — a ₹85 lakh increase. His declared occupation is politics; no significant business income is noted. The growth is above the legitimate salary threshold and warrants scrutiny, though it is far below the most extreme cases.
spotlights.source: ECI affidavit data 2021 & 2026; ADR India MyNeta.

spotlights.name: Haripada Barman
spotlights.desc: Haripada Barman declared ₹42.6 lakh in 2021 and ₹1.76 crore in 2026 — the single largest percentage increase in this tracker's dataset. The primary driver: a dramatic expansion in gold holdings, with approximately 490 grams of gold declared in 2026. An MLA salary over five years accounts for at most ₹40–50 lakh; the remaining ₹93 lakh increase has no declared business or professional income source on file.

spotlights.name: Hiranmoy Chattopadhyay
spotlights.desc: With ₹12.89 crore declared in 2026, Hiranmoy Chattopadhyay holds the highest absolute wealth among all MLAs in this tracker. His investment profile is unusually sophisticated for a state legislator: over 60 distinct stock market holdings declared in the 2026 affidavit, alongside significant bank balances. The ₹7.09 crore increase in five years — while having no declared prior political office — warrants scrutiny of the income sources underpinning this portfolio.

spotlights.name: Sadhan Ghosh
spotlights.desc: Sadhan Ghosh nearly tripled his declared assets from ₹82.1 lakh to ₹2.4 crore between 2021 and 2026. Unusually, he also declared ₹75 lakh in liabilities — the highest debt-to-asset ratio among tracked MLAs. The combination of rapid asset accumulation and substantial outstanding liabilities raises questions about the financial arrangements underpinning both the growth and the debt.

spotlights.name: Firhad Hakim
spotlights.role: TMC · Kolkata Port MLA · Kolkata Mayor · Narada sting accused
spotlights.desc: Firhad Hakim declared ₹13.20 crore in 2021 and ₹22.57 crore in 2026 — a ₹9.37 crore increase while serving as a state minister and Kolkata's Mayor. He is also a named accused in the Narada sting case, captured on video accepting cash in 2014, and was arrested by the CBI in May 2021 (bailed the same day by Calcutta HC). The 71% asset growth while facing an active criminal trial — and while holding the office of Mayor — is among the highest declared growth rates for a senior minister in this tracker. He was one of only 80 TMC MLAs to survive the 2026 BJP wave, winning Kolkata Port by a margin of 27,443 votes.
spotlights.source: ADR / West Bengal Election Watch, 2026. CBI Narada case records.

spotlights.name: Anubrata Mondal ("Kesto")
spotlights.role: TMC · Bolpur MLA (did not contest 2026 — arrested)
spotlights.desc: Anubrata Mondal declared modest assets across elections — ₹1.62 crore in 2019, ₹1.81 crore in 2021. The ED, probing his role in the cattle trafficking network, attached properties and bank accounts far exceeding his declared assets. He did not contest the 2026 election, having been arrested in 2022 and spending 17 months in custody. His daughter Sukanya Mondal was separately arrested in an assets-related probe.
spotlights.source: ADR India affidavit database; ED attachment orders 2022–23.

partyAverages.note: ADR/WB Election Watch 2026: ₹1.94 Cr avg (2021) → ₹2.97 Cr avg (2026); pulled up by a small number of very wealthy MLAs; median is substantially lower
ADR/WB Election Watch 2026: ₹3.48 Cr avg (2021) → ₹5.36 Cr avg (2026); higher average reflects 14 years of incumbency and ministerial wealth accumulation
Estimated from ECI affidavit sample; ADR aggregate not separately published for CPIM 2026 cohort. Party discipline historically suppresses declared wealth.

ADR/WB Election Watch 2026: ₹11.20 Cr avg (2021) → ₹17.92 Cr avg (2026). INC won only 2 seats (Sujapur, Hansan); high average reflects wealthy urban/zamindar-background candidates in their winning pockets.

macroStats.source: ADR / West Bengal Election Watch, 2026 Assembly Election
